title: Old-Fashioned Onion Soup (Community Enhanced)
original_ingredients: 7 slices French or Italian bread, cut into 1 inch cubes
original_instructions: Place sliced onions and butter into slow cooker, and mix until onions are thoroughly coated. Stir in bread and chicken broth.
Cover, and cook on LOW for 10 to 18 hours or on HIGH 4 to 5 hours, stirring occasionally. Stir well during last hour.
ingredients: 7 slices French or Italian bread, cut into 1 inch cubes
instructions: Place sliced onions and butter into slow cooker, and mix until onions are thoroughly coated. Stir in bread and chicken broth.
Cover, and cook on LOW for 10 to 18 hours or on HIGH 4 to 5 hours, stirring occasionally. Stir well during last hour.

line_diffs.reasoning: Adjusting onion and broth ratio for personal taste preferences
Using chicken stock instead of chicken broth may add more depth of flavor

source_review.text: Used Chicken Stock instead of Broth (Costco), tore up the slices of bread. I think next time I'll quarter the slices of onion. It was a little messy to eat.

modifications_applied.reasoning: Using chicken stock instead of chicken broth may add more depth of flavor

source_review.text: This is a good starter recipe. Next time I make it I will use a bit less onion andquite a bit more broth. The changes I already made were to use beef broth instead of chicken, and I didn't add the bread while it cooked. I added the bread to the bowls and put provolone cheese on top. Delicious!

modifications_applied.reasoning: Adjusting onion and broth ratio for personal taste preferences

enhancement_summary.expected_impact: Using chicken stock instead of chicken broth may add more depth of flavor; Adjusting onion and broth ratio for personal taste preferences

description: This recipe requires only four ingredients (onions, butter, French bread, and chicken broth), but you need to start simmering this soup ten to eighteen hours before you plan to serve it.
